headline: The man behind the legendary MPC, Roger Linn, stays focused with a single browser tab
summary: Roger Linn, creator of the iconic LM-1 and LinnDrum, is a music technology pioneer. He's renowned for inventing the first sample-based drum machine. The article delves into his unique focus and creative process.

headline: Here are 38 Memorial Day deals we recommend for $50 or less
summary: Discover 38 fantastic tech deals available for under $50 this Memorial Day. Find budget-friendly gadgets like portable chargers and 4K streaming devices. Skip the big-ticket items for these smart, affordable tech buys.

headline: Hanging out in my favorite virtual coffee shop in Tokyo
summary: Experience the unique comfort of a virtual Tokyo coffee shop through a gaming lens. The author shares a revelatory experience finding a digital space that feels like home. This piece explores the charm and appeal of virtual hangouts in games like Coffee Talk.

headline: I have a new go-to browser
summary: The author reveals their new favorite web browser, Vivaldi, in the latest 'Installer' column. They discuss the features and reasons for making this switch. Discover why this particular browser has earned a prime spot in their digital routine.

headline: Resident Evil Movie Director Says Fans Would Be 'Bummed' If He 'Just Did the Story of the Games'
summary: Resident Evil movie director Zach Cregger explains his creative choices. He argues that a direct adaptation of game stories would disappoint fans. Cregger aims to deliver a fresh narrative while honoring the franchise.

headline: Vampyros Lesbos/She Killed in Ecstasy Review
summary: This review covers stunning 4K treatments of Jess Franco's films. Dive into 'Vampyros Lesbos' and 'She Killed in Ecstasy' with enhanced visuals. Discover the enduring legacy of these cult classics in a new format.

headline: The Mandalorian and Grogu: Did the New Republic Commit War Crimes?
summary: The upcoming 'Mandalorian and Grogu' movie sparks a debate. A key plot point questions if the New Republic committed war crimes. Explore the moral complexities introduced in the new Star Wars film.

headline: Warhammer 40,000 Is an Accessibility Battle
summary: Warhammer 40,000 presents significant accessibility challenges for disabled players. Issues range from the physical demands of rolling dice to painting miniatures. The article advocates for greater inclusivity within the tabletop hobby.

headline: France suspends extra EU border checks at Dover after hours-long queues
summary: France has temporarily suspended additional EU border checks at Dover. The decision followed hours-long queues and soaring temperatures at the port. This move aims to alleviate travel delays for passengers.

headline: Hottest day of year so far as temperatures soar on bank holiday weekend
summary: The UK experienced its hottest day of the year over the bank holiday weekend. Soaring temperatures led to amber heat health alerts across parts of England. Residents in the Midlands, eastern, and south-east England are advised to take precautions.

headline: 'Shameful' more spent on benefits than jobs for young people, says Milburn
summary: Alan Milburn criticizes the UK's welfare system spending priorities. He argues more funds go to benefits than creating youth employment. Milburn calls for reforms to better support young people not in work or education.

headline: French mum and partner held until trial after sons abandoned by road in Portugal
summary: A French mother and her partner are being held in custody in Portugal. They face trial after allegedly abandoning the woman's sons by the roadside. The incident occurred in southern Portugal, sparking a legal investigation.
